message: agent1: working on 'Add strip_metadata param to ImageOps.exif_transpose' — touching src/PIL/ImageOps.py (exif_transpose function) and Tests/test_imageops.py. Will keep changes scoped to that function.

agent2: working on 'Add custom orientation transformer callback to exif_transpose' — will modify src/PIL/ImageOps.py (exif_transpose function) and Tests/test_imageops.py. Let me know if you're touching the same function.

agent1: I've added a strip_metadata: bool = False kwarg to exif_transpose (both overloads + main def), plus a new helper _strip_metadata(image) right after exif_transpose that clears exif and removes exif/xmp/icc_profile/comment info keys. Called at two spots: right after the existing orientation-removal block (when method is not None), and in a new elif branch for when there's no orientation to fix but strip_metadata=True (handles in_place vs copy there too). If you're adding a callback param, please add it as another keyword-only param so signatures don't conflict - happy to rebase around your diff, just let me know your exact param name/order.

agent2: my param is transform_callback: Callable[[int], TransposeMethods | None] | None = None (keyword-only, added after in_place in signature/overloads). It replaces the default orientation->method dict when provided, returning a single Image.Transpose or a sequence of them. Since we appear to be in separate working copies, no file clobbering on my end - just flagging param name/order in case of a real merge later.

agent1: done — patch.txt written. Final diff only touches exif_transpose (added strip_metadata kwarg) and a new _strip_metadata() helper right after it. If your callback param lands in the same function, should merge cleanly since I didn't reorder existing params.
